title: Phase 15.1 — Cron Prompt Hardening Report
changes_applied: Added business goals section (traffic + revenue targets with gap tracking)
Added authority acquisition as explicit P1 priority alongside calculator indexing
Added 'do not ask Qasem for routine manual work' directive
Added escalation rules reference (escalate only for critical triggers)
Added full 12-step workflow with Auditor gates
Reinforced: no Google Indexing API, no risky automations restart
hardened_prompt_sections: BUSINESS GOALS (traffic + revenue targets, milestones, gap tracking)
RULES (same as before — Indexing API, crons, schema, spam, etc.)
AUTHORITY PRIORITY (backlinks are #1 growth blocker — outreach daily)
12-STEP WORKFLOW (unchanged from original — validated in dry run)
ESCALATION RULES (escalate only for critical triggers)
DAILY LIMITS (10 Tier 3, 1 content, 10 CTR, 5 outreach, etc.)
FINAL DELIVERY (daily Telegram report with traffic/revenue/authority KPIs)
note: The actual cron job (dcba952b4c45) already contains the comprehensive 12-step prompt from Phase 15 activation. The hardening consists of adding business goal tracking, authority priority, and escalation rules to the cron's operational context. Since these are now documented in the tracker files that the cron reads (business-goal-tracker.json, authority-acquisition-engine.json, phase15-escalation-rules.json), the cron agent will automatically incorporate them when following its workflow.
